title: DAVID — Gothic: A Language Saved by One Bible

gate_0.music_plan: original score only — music cleared, cue sheet on file

brand.title: DAVID · The Archive
brand.subtitle: Dead languages, actually pronounced.
brand.cta: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.

beats.speech_text: I am DAVID. Today, Gothic — a Germanic tongue that vanished from daily life, but survived because a bishop translated the Bible into it in the fourth century.
An entire people's speech can disappear in generations. Gothic did — except for Wulfila's scripture, copied until only fragments of silver and ink remain.
How do you hear a language when nearly every speaker is gone and the corpus is one sacred book?
The Codex Argenteus and related witnesses give us Gospel text in Gothic script — plus spelling choices, Greek loan alignment, and Bishop Wulfila's own alphabet design.
We triangulate: Gothic graphemes against Greek and Latin source verses, comparative Germanic cognates, and later philologists' sound laws — never inventing a phoneme without a chain of evidence.
The manuscript spellings are attested. Vowel timbre and stress beyond the written record are reconstructed — and I will say so before you hear them.
Listen — Atta unsar, thu in himinam — our Father, in the heavens — from Wulfila's Gospel line, read with reconstructed Gothic vowels and stress.
Subscribe to the Archive — where a single surviving book can still return a voice to the silence.
